name: memory-engine
description: Captures user prompts and agent responses to Memory Engine for persistent context across sessions

api_key.title: API key (optional)
api_key.description: Optional. Leave blank to use your `me login` session — the plugin falls back to it automatically. Set an API key to attribute captures to a dedicated agent instead: create one with `me apikey create --agent <agent>` (or have an admin create one with restricted privileges).

server.title: Server URL (optional)
server.description: Optional. Leave blank to use your `me login` server — the plugin falls back to your CLI config, same as the api key and space. Set it to pin a specific server, e.g. for unattended or project-scope installs (e.g. https://api.memory.build).

space.title: Space (optional)
space.description: Space slug to capture into (the X-Me-Space). Leave blank to use your active space (`me space use` / ME_SPACE). Pin it for unattended or project-scope installs so captures always land in the same space; if blank and no active space is set, captures are skipped.

tree_root.title: Tree root
tree_root.description: Ltree root under which captures are nested as `<root>.<project>.agent_sessions` (the same layout `me ... import` uses, so live and imported sessions share a node per project). Defaults under `share` so your login session can write there; use a `~`-prefixed root to keep captures in your private home instead.

content_mode.title: Content mode
content_mode.description: What to capture per message. `default` stores the user + assistant text (recommended). `full_transcript` also stores reasoning and tool calls/results as their own memories — more complete but much larger/noisier (and may include sensitive tool output).
